description: Failure taxonomy — standardized categories for all failure records. Every testament, Ecclesiastes report, and flood post-mortem must tag itself with one or more categories. The Judges pattern matcher searches by category.

categories.name: Canon Violation
categories.description: An agent broke a Canon rule. The fix is internal — follow the rule.

categories.name: Systemic Futility
categories.description: The system followed every rule and still failed. The mandate was wrong, the environment was hostile, or the output was correct and useless.

categories.name: Mandate Ambiguity
categories.description: The mandate was unclear enough that agents interpreted it differently. The fix is better mandate definition.

categories.name: Environmental Change
categories.description: The context changed during execution — dependencies shifted, requirements evolved, external systems changed. Not agent error.

categories.name: Capability Degradation (The Fall)
categories.description: An agent that was previously reliable produced lower-quality output. Drift, not a sudden break.

categories.name: Scope Creep (Babel)
categories.description: The mandate expanded beyond its original boundaries without authorization. Complexity grew for its own sake.

categories.name: Overlap Conflict (Cain & Abel)
categories.description: Two agents produced contradictory work on the same domain without coordination.

categories.name: Handoff Loss
categories.description: Information was lost or degraded when passing between agents. The epistle was incomplete or the edge cases were not flagged.

categories.name: Wrong Goal (Jeremiah)
categories.description: The mandate was technically executed but the goal itself was misguided. Jeremiah was right.

note: Failure records are appended here by agents at sunset (in testaments) and by Ecclesiastes (in reports). The Judges pattern matcher searches records by category to detect recurring patterns.
